response:
a freelance graphic designer and typographer is working on a project that involves creating a promotional poster for an upcoming exhibition. inspired by coy! communications and mark denton, the designer decides to incorporate a blend of traditional art forms and modern marketing techniques. the poster will feature a central circular design surrounded by a series of concentric circles, each representing different marketing elements. the central circle has a radius of $ r $ cm. the next circle has a radius that is 10% larger than the previous one, and this pattern continues for a total of 8 concentric circles. calculate the total area covered by all 8 circles combined. express your answer in terms of $ r $ and $ \pi $.  

the area of a circle is $ \pi r^2 $. the area of the first circle is $ \pi r^2 $. the second circle has a radius that is 10% larger than $ r $, so its radius is $ 1.1r $. the area of the second circle is $ \pi (1.1r)^2 = \pi (1.21r^2) $. the third circle has a radius that is 10% larger than $ 1.1r $, so its radius is $ 1.21r $. the area of the third circle is $ \pi (1.21r)^2 = \pi (1.4641r^2) $. this pattern continues for 8 circles. the total area is the sum of the areas of all 8 circles.  

the total area covered by all 8 circles combined is $ \pi r^2 + \pi (1.21r^2) + \pi (1.4641r^2) + \pi (1.771561r^2) + \pi (2.073461r^2) + \pi (2.400427r^2) + \pi (2.760469r^2) + \pi (3.156516r^2) $.  

to add a touch of traditional typography, the designer decides to place text along the circumference of the 5th circle. if the circumference of the 5th circle is used to evenly space text characters such that there is a 0.5 cm gap between each character, and the total number of characters (including spaces) must be
